label: AppleScript • live create-if-missing
query: In Notes, put the provided body into a note named 'Sprint Log', creating the note if it doesn't already exist.
notes: Capability lane for the A3 find-or-create rule: the target note is absent from the mock 'app world' (empty seed), so a script that blindly addresses `note "Sprint Log"` would be wrong on a real Mac. Scores that the real 16B handles the possibly-missing object — creating it or otherwise resolving it — instead of assuming it exists (Grok rubric), and that it used the {{content}} placeholder for the body. No side effects. Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

appleScript.content:
Sprint 14 log:
- Landed identifier fidelity
- Started the capability sweep
rubric: The AppleScript ensures the note named 'Sprint Log' exists — creating it (e.g. with `make new note`) or otherwise handling the case where it is absent — rather than assuming it is already there.
It sets that note's body to the provided content and targets the Notes application.
